diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers kept the same frozen unsupported label and used no citations, so there is no citation difference to resolve. The main difference is the reason code choice: option 1 uses a more claim-directed code, central_proposition_unsupported, which better matches the anchor proposition 'reuse_old_fix' in light of the evidence bundle showing the prior fix worked at one point but the bug was later reopened and a new root cause was identified. Option 2's insufficient_evidence is broader and less precise because the available evidence does more than merely omit support; it also undercuts the proposition's applicability to the current situation. The rationales are compatible rather than conflicting: one emphasizes contradiction/inapplicability from reopening and new root cause, while the other emphasizes lack of evidence that reusing the old fix would now be appropriate. Confidence does not differ because both are high.
